# Pull Request

## Description

Briefly describe the changes made in this PR.

**Example:**
This PR adds support for converting between JSON5 and standard JSON formats. It includes a new parser that handles JSON5 syntax (trailing commas, unquoted keys, etc.) and comprehensive tests.

## Type of Change

Mark the relevant option with an `x`.

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Tests (adding or updating tests)

## Related Issues

Fixes #(issue number)

Related to #(issue number)

## How Has This Been Tested?

Please describe the tests that you ran to verify your changes.

**Example:**
- Unit tests for the new JSON5 parser
- Integration tests for round-trip conversion
- Manual testing with sample files
- Tested on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12

## Test Coverage

- [ ] All new code is covered by tests
- [ ] Coverage is maintained at 80% or higher
- [ ] Tests pass locally: `pytest tests/ -v`

## Documentation

- [ ] Code is well-documented with docstrings
- [ ] New features are documented in `docs/`
- [ ] Examples are updated in `examples/`
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (for user-facing changes)

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing tests pass locally
- [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md if this is a user-facing change

## Additional Notes

Any additional information or context that reviewers should know.
